# @platform/session-runtime

Session service: server-side session storage, token binding, session lifecycle, and claims propagation.

## Package identity

```text
Name: @platform/session-runtime
Version: 0.1.0
Component: session-runtime
Type: library
System: enterprise-react-platform
Domain: operations
Bounded context: session-service
Owner: team-security
```

## Lifecycle

```text
Stage: active
Role: platform
Class: active.platform
Catalog lifecycle: production
Visibility: internal
Support level: standard
Review cadence: quarterly
```

## Ownership

```text
Owner: team-security
Change control: owner-review
Promotion eligible: true
Semver policy: internal-traceable
```

## Responsibilities

- Store and retrieve session state backed by Redis
- Bind authentication tokens to server-side session records
- Propagate user claims and roles through the request context
- Manage session expiry, rotation, and revocation

## Non-responsibilities

- Does not implement the authentication protocol (owned by security-auth)
- Does not own the Redis connection or cluster configuration (adapters-redis)
- Does not expose session data to client-side JavaScript

## Public exports and usage

```text
Public exports only: true
Deep imports allowed: false
```

- Imported by the API runtime middleware pipeline to attach session context to requests
- Application packages access user claims via the session context accessor

platform

## Relations

### Depends on

```text
@platform/adapters-redis
```

### Provides APIs

```text
session-service
session-context
claims-accessor
```

### Consumes APIs

```text
auth-gateway
cache-queue-redis
```

## Operational notes

- Session expiry must be aligned with IdP token lifetimes
- Redis key namespacing must isolate sessions from queue data
